headline: Commerce Dept. quietly withdraws proposed rule on global AI chip exports
summary: Investing.com — The U.S. Department of Commerce has abruptly withdrawn a planned rule aimed at tightening global exports of advanced artificial intelligence chips, according to a government website posting on Friday. The draft regulation, which had been under interagency review since late February, was widely expected to serve as a cornerstone in the administration’s strategy to govern the international distribution of high-end semiconductor technology.

content.title: Commerce Dept. quietly withdraws proposed rule on global AI chip exports
content.summary: Investing.com — The U.S. Department of Commerce has abruptly withdrawn a planned rule aimed at tightening global exports of advanced artificial intelligence chips, according to a government website posting on Friday. The draft regulation, which had been under interagency review since late February, was widely expected to serve as a cornerstone in the administration’s strategy to govern the international distribution of high-end semiconductor technology.

headline: NVIDIA Joins OCI To Shape Optical Links And AI Data Center Future
summary: NVIDIA (NasdaqGS:NVDA) has co founded the Optical Compute Interconnect (OCI) Multi Source Agreement to define open standards for optical links in AI data centers. The consortium includes AMD, Broadcom, Meta, Microsoft, and OpenAI, aiming to create interoperable, high density optical interconnects. The group intends to address copper connectivity bottlenecks and support much larger AI clusters for future workloads. NVIDIA enters this effort with a strong market profile and a current share...

Cramer noted that the company is a better performer than Coherent, as he said: If you’re looking for groups to buy into weakness as rising oil prices crush the stock market, you can do a lot worse […]
